```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  // idempotence: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

**Explanation of the `ensures` clauses:**

- `kind` and `value` never change — the canonical id refers to the same entity.
- `width` is `x.width` if already ≥ 4 (returned unchanged), otherwise exactly `PAD` (left-zero-padded to a minimum of 4 digits).
- The result width is always ≥ `PAD` (the canonical-width guarantee).
- The result is `Wellformed` (its width can still hold its value).
- Idempotence: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op.

Note the `width` clause is the tight specification; the other clauses (`>= PAD`, `Wellformed`, idempotence) are derivable consequences included for completeness/auditing.
